title: Edge Security, WAF, Headers, and Origin Containment · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production board compares near-miss TypeScript and AWS decisions for edge security, waf, headers, and origin containment and accepts only runtime validation, bounded ownership, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit controlled AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for layered edge-security plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, apply WAF, rate limits, bot controls, request size and method rules with false-positive, cost, accessibility, and bypass evidence.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, treat Referer hotlink controls as a weak cost signal and use signed access or a controlled origin header only within its actual threat boundary.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, set HSTS, MIME sniffing, frame, referrer, content security, cross-origin, and content-disposition headers appropriate to file types and embedding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Origin Shield Error Containment
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 8480 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to exhaustively evaluate application, WAF, origin, header, and private-error controls; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then change region, retrieval, or transfer cost and defend the revised budget action. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, use Origin Shield, request collapsing, failover, bounded error caching, stale delivery, and load shedding without hiding recovery or serving private content.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Origin Shield Error Containment.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, use origin shield, request collapsing, failover, bounded error caching, stale delivery, and load shedding without hiding recovery or serving private content. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Origin Shield Error Containment, keep Origin Shield Error Containment connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.
